studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The primary outcome is collected at enrollment, after 3 weeks, 1 day pre surgery, 2 weeks post surgery, 6 weeks post surgery, 10 weeks post surgery, and 14 weeks post surgery.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention: Standard of care + Use of the app; Control: Standard of care
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Arm 1 received standard of care plus the Orthopy app, a smartphone application providing individually tailored content and training for orthopedic injuries. Arm 2 received standard of care only.
studies.collected_data: KOOS (knee injury and osteoarthritis outcome score), Kinesiophobia (TAMPA scale), subjective pain level (numeric VAS), subjective function (numeric VAS), subjective patient satisfaction (numeric VAS), physical therapy/lymphatic drainage needed (yes/no), ability to work (yes/no), patient-relevant structural and procedural improvements (pSVV), thigh circumference, lower leg circumference, patellar center, angular measurements (range of motion), stability test (anteroposterior stability of the knee joint), coordination, quick strength, agility, and custom-developed questions on app use.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: The interventional group showed positive outcomes on the KOOS Pain and Symptoms subscale compared to the standard of care group. Statistically significant pain reduction was seen at t1 (p = 0.0005), t2 (p = 0.0068) and t4 (approximately 6 weeks after surgery; p = 0.01). Significant reduction of symptoms at t1 (p = 0.0001), t2 (p = 0.0036) and t6 (p = 0.0377) were recognized. These findings are further supported by the significant benefit of the pain NRS at t4 and positive tendencies at t3, t5 and t6. The KOOS subscales quality of life (p = 0.0066), activities of daily living (p = 0.0009) and Sport (p = 0.0484) showed significant effects before surgery.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention period lasts at least 15 weeks, consisting of a preoperative phase of at least 1 week and a postoperative phase of 14 weeks.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention group: 164 patients; Control group: 164 patients
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group: Receives access to the Orthopy App in addition to usual care (Add-on). The app provides video-based home training and educational content for the pre- and postoperative phases, to be used 3 times a week. Control group: Receives usual care (UC), consisting of routine medical treatment and physiotherapy prescriptions (essentially 6 sessions of physical therapy).
studies.collected_data: Subjective pain intensity (VAS Pain); Health-related quality of life and functional limitations (WOMET); Subjective perception of pain intensity (KOOS subscale pain); Subjective perception of symptoms and functional deficits (KOOS subscale symptoms and function); Objective assessment of proprioceptive abilities (angle position sense test / angle reproduction test); Range of motion (ROM via goniometer).

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention period lasts at least 18 weeks, consisting of a preoperative phase of at least 4 weeks and a postoperative phase of 14 weeks. Assessments occur at T0 (28-36 days pre-op), T1 (1 day pre-op), T2 (3 days post-op), T3 (2 weeks post-op), T4 (6 weeks post-op), T5 (10 weeks post-op), and T6 (14 weeks post-op).
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention Group: Receives access to the Orthopy App in addition to usual care (Add-on). The app provides video-based home training and educational content for the pre- and postoperative phases, to be used 3 times a week. Control Group: Receives usual care, consisting of routine medical treatment and physiotherapy prescriptions (essentially 18 sessions of physical therapy and 6 sessions of manual lymphatic drainage).
studies.collected_data: KOOS subscale – Symptoms and Function (Primary Outcome); KOOS-ACL subscale – Function; VAS Pain; LERAS-13; KOOS subscale – QoL; Angle Reproduction test (proprioceptive abilities); Range of Motion (via goniometer).

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention period is at least 16 weeks, consisting of a preoperative phase of at least 2 weeks and a postoperative phase of 14 weeks. Data is collected at baseline (T0), preoperatively (T1), and at 2, 6, and 10 weeks post-surgery (T2, T3, T4), with a final survey at the end of the intervention period (T5).
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention group: Pre- and postoperative use of a smartphone app (Orthopy App) in addition to standard of care. Control group: Standard of care (regular physiotherapy).
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group uses the Orthopy app in addition to standard treatment. The control group receives standard treatment only, which essentially provides for the prescription of physiotherapy, with supplementary care (analgesics, lymphatic drainage, orthoses, motion splints) possible.
studies.collected_data: Primary Outcome: KOOS (Knee injury and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score) Subscale Symptoms and functional limitations. Secondary Outcomes: KOOS Pain subscale, Subjective pain level (numerical VAS), Subjective function (numerical VAS), KOOS Activities of Daily Living subscale, KOOS Quality of Life subscale, KOOS Sport subscale. Exploratory endpoints: thigh circumference, range of motion (in extension, neutral position, and flexion). Prescribed and utilized services of standard therapy are also recorded.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Each participant will be involved in the study for about 12 weeks, which corresponds to the usual follow-up period after ACL surgery.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Group 1: Combination therapy (n=15); Group 2: Shockwave therapy only (n=15); Group 3: Cryotherapy only (n=15); Group 4: Control group with standardized physiotherapy only (n=15).
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Group 1 receives combined ESWT and cryotherapy; Group 2 receives ESWT alone; Group 3 receives cryotherapy alone; Group 4 receives standardized physiotherapy only. For Groups 1 and 3, cryotherapy starts on the day of surgery (5 times within the first 48 hours). For Groups 1 and 2, weekly shockwave therapy starts on the second postoperative day for 5 weeks. All participants receive structured physiotherapy and use the 'Orthopy' app starting in the third postoperative week.
studies.collected_data: IKDC score (at 1, 3, 6, and 12 weeks postoperatively); Tegner activity scores, Lysholm scores, VAS pain scores, swelling status, and range of motion (at 1, 3, 6, and 12 weeks postoperatively); patient satisfaction; and rehabilitation progress documented through the Orthopy mobile application.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The study included a presurgery period (2-6 weeks) and a postsurgery period (14 weeks). Assessments were conducted at baseline (t0), presurgery (t1, t2), and postsurgery (t3, t4 at approx 6 weeks, t5, and t6 at 14 weeks).
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention: App-assisted therapy plus standard of care; Control: Isolated standard of care.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group received the Orthopy app in addition to standard of care (conventional orthopaedic and physiotherapeutic measures). The control group received isolated standard of care.
studies.collected_data: Knee injury and osteoarthritis outcome score (KOOS) including subscales for Pain, Symptoms, Quality of life, Activities of daily living, and Sport; subjective pain and function levels via numeric rating scale (NRS).
studies.key_outcomes_findings: The interventional group showed statistically significant pain reduction at t1 (p=0.0005), t2 (p=0.0068), and t4 (approximately 6 weeks after surgery; p=0.01). A significant reduction of symptoms was seen at t1 (p=0.0001), t2 (p=0.0036), and t6 (p=0.0377). There was a significant benefit on the pain NRS at t4. KOOS subscales for quality of life (p=0.0066), activities of daily living (p=0.0009), and Sport (p=0.0484) showed significant effects before surgery. Combining the Orthopy app and standard of care significantly improved prehabilitation and rehabilitation compared with isolated standard of care.

audit.study_title: Use of a smartphone application (Orthopy App) to improve preoperative and postoperative therapy in patients with ACL rupture compared to conventional standard of care treatment - a randomized, monocentric study.

Investigation of the effectiveness of integrated care using the Orthopy app in addition to usual care compared to usual care alone in patients with meniscal lesions – a randomized, controlled, multicenter trial
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Investigation of the effectiveness of integrated care using the Orthopy app in addition to usual care compared to usual care alone in patients with anterior cruciate ligament rupture (with and without meniscal lesions) – a randomized, controlled, multicenter trial

Use of a smartphone application (Orthopy App) to improve pre- and
postoperative therapy in patients after anterior cruciate ligament rupture

multicenter study

audit.study_title: Early Postoperative Application of Radial Extracorporeal Shock Wave Therapy (rESWT) and Neuroreflectory Hyberbaric CO2 Cryotherapie Following Primary Reconstruction of the Anterior Cruciate Ligament: A Prospective, Randomized, Controlled Study
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Combining a digital health application with standard care significantly enhances rehabilitation outcomes for ACL surgery patients.
